pr.title: cm: update cm_80452C68.mode usages to use CameraType enum
pr.author: sadkellz

summary: Replaced raw numeric assignments and comparisons for cm_80452C68.mode in src/melee/cm/camera.c with named CameraType enum constants such as CAMERA_STANDARD, CAMERA_BOSS_INTRO, CAMERA_FREE, CAMERA_DEBUG_FOLLOW, and CAMERA_DEBUG_FREE. The change is behavior-preserving and improves readability/type clarity for camera mode logic.
what_changed: Changed cm_80452C68.mode initialization from 0 to CAMERA_STANDARD in Camera_80028B9C.

Changed repeated boss-intro checks from mode != 6 to mode != CAMERA_BOSS_INTRO across camera transition/helper functions including Camera_8002E6FC, Camera_8002E818, Camera_8002E948, Camera_8002EA64, Camera_8002EB5C, Camera_8002EC7C, Camera_8002ED9C, Camera_8002EEC8, and Camera_8002F274.

Changed mode-setting helpers from raw values to enum constants: CAMERA_STANDARD, CAMERA_TRAINING_MENU, CAMERA_CLEAR, CAMERA_FIXED, CAMERA_FREE, CAMERA_BOSS_INTRO, CAMERA_DEBUG_FOLLOW, and CAMERA_DEBUG_FREE.
Changed debug camera guards from raw comparisons like mode != 7 and mode != 8 to CAMERA_DEBUG_FOLLOW and CAMERA_DEBUG_FREE.
Preserved existing casts where needed for matching or type compatibility, such as cm_80452C68.mode <= (u32)CAMERA_PAUSE and comparisons like (int) cm_80452C68.mode == CAMERA_FREE.
Only src/melee/cm/camera.c changed, with 27 added and 27 deleted lines across 23 hunks.
smart_moves: Converted magic numbers to semantic enum names without changing control flow or line count materially, making this a low-risk decomp readability improvement.
Kept explicit casts around enum comparisons where the original code used unsigned or int comparisons, which helps preserve compiler behavior and matching constraints.
Targeted only usages of cm_80452C68.mode rather than broad unrelated camera cleanup, keeping the PR focused and easy to verify.
Used enum names that document gameplay/debug camera states directly at call sites, making future camera decomp work easier to reason about.
decomp_lessons: When a struct field has an established enum type, replace raw mode constants at assignment and comparison sites to improve semantic clarity while preserving generated code.
Enum adoption can be done incrementally in one subsystem file, especially when the diff is mechanically verifiable as numeric constant replacement.
Do not remove casts blindly during enum cleanup; casts such as to u32 or int may be necessary for matching signedness or original comparison codegen.
Repeated magic constants are strong evidence for named mode values; replacing all related instances in one pass reduces ambiguity in future reverse-engineering.
naming_conventions: Camera mode enum constants use an all-caps CAMERA_* style.
Observed CameraType values include CAMERA_STANDARD, CAMERA_PAUSE, CAMERA_TRAINING_MENU, CAMERA_CLEAR, CAMERA_FIXED, CAMERA_FREE, CAMERA_BOSS_INTRO, CAMERA_DEBUG_FOLLOW, and CAMERA_DEBUG_FREE.
Existing decomp symbol names were left unchanged, including cm_80452C68, cm_80453004, Camera_8002FE38, Camera_8003010C, Camera_80030130, Camera_80030154, and Camera_80030178.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This appears to be a matching-safe semantic replacement of numeric constants with enum constants.
The diff preserves comparison structure and casts, including cm_80452C68.mode <= (u32)CAMERA_PAUSE and (int) cm_80452C68.mode == CAMERA_FREE/BOSS_INTRO/DEBUG_FOLLOW/DEBUG_FREE.
No assembly changes, explicit nonmatching notes, or reviewer matching discussion were present in the provided PR slice.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided context.
The PR was merged quickly on 2026-01-05, but the provided slice does not include reviewer rationale.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining cm_80452C68.mode raw numeric comparisons in camera-related files.
Check where CameraType is defined and whether cm_80452C68.mode is typed as CameraType in the camera globals/struct header.
Search for other camera state fields with magic numeric modes that can be converted to named enums.
Verify whether casts around CameraType comparisons are still required for matching after broader type cleanup.
